mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which sustains corporate revenue expansion and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Sharpe ratio or rank correlation below 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization that supports near-term corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during recovery phases or rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation readings above 3.5% annualized force monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, increasing equity discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive returns occur in > 60% of high-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative or below 0.02 across all 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic expansion that lifts equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 50 bps annually or shows negative predictive accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing activity that reduces near-term corporate cash flow expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO drops below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or rank IC < 0.01.
